revision.comment: Merging [[:Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 745]] to [[:Category:745 establishments]] and [[:Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] per [[Wikipedia:Categories for discussion/Log/2024 June 20]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == Images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}}

The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == Images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[en:Sagami-ji]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Articles tagged as templates and modules]] [[en:Sagami-ji]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == Images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[en:Sagami-ji]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Articles tagged as templates and modules]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Sagami-ji]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[en:Sagami-ji]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}}

Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == Images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Sagami-ji]] [[en:Sagami-ji]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Sagami-ji]] [[en:Sagami-ji]]

The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Sagami-ji]] [[en:Sagami-ji]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Sagami-ji]] [[en:Sagami-ji]]

The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Sagami-ji]] [[en:Sagami-ji]]

{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Hyogo Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Sagami-ji]] [[en:Sagami-ji]]

The building was badly damaged in the [[Heiji Rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[:en:Bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] }} [[sn:Sagami-ji]] [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

Inscriptions on temple [[:en:Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[Jūichimen Kannon]] | country = [[Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[:en:Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[shōgun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[:en:Honda Tadamasa|Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[:en:Bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] }} [[sn:Sagami-ji]] [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[:en:Honda Tadamasa|Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[:en:Bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] }} [[sn:Sagami-ji]] [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[:en:Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[:en:Honda Tadamasa|Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[:en:Bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] }} [[sn:Sagami-ji]] [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}}

The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[:en:Honda Tadamasa|Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[:en:Bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] }} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}}

[[arz:ساجامى جى]][[sn:commons:Category:Sagamiji]][[de:Sagami-ji]][[en:Sagami-ji]][[es:Sagami-ji]][[fr:Sagami-ji]][[ja:酒見寺]][[tr:Sagami-ji]][[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]][[zh:酒見寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Sagamiji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182}}|prefecture={{ill|Kasai|WD=Q851155}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.9357|N|134.82959167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664}}|deity={{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3137229]] Wikidata name: Sagami-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[:en:Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[:en:Honda Tadamasa|Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[:en:Bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] }} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}}

The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[:en:Honda Tadamasa|Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[:en:Bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] }} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[:en:Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[:en:Honda Tadamasa|Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[:en:Bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] }} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[:en:Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[:en:Honda Tadamasa|Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[:en:Bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] }} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[:en:Honda Tadamasa|Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[:en:Bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai-shi]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo-ken]], 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] | country = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhist]] temple in [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era) at the request of [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]], a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] received an oracle from a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]], {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple [[:en:Commemorative plaque|plaques]] record later visits from various [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperors]] and ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]s'', including ''shōgun'' [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]]. The building was badly damaged in the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji rebellion]] of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji]], [[:en:Honda Tadamasa|Honda Tadamasa]], agreed to aid the [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|[[:en:Rōmon|Rōmon]] File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The [[:en:Bracket (architecture)|bracket]]s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Inscriptions on temple {{ill|Commemorative plaque|en|Commemorative plaque|WD=Q721747|lt=plaques}} record later visits from various {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperors}} and ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}s'', including ''shōgun'' {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. The building was badly damaged in the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji rebellion}} of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji}}, {{ill|Honda Tadamasa|en|Honda Tadamasa|WD=Q3275929|lt=Honda Tadamasa}}, agreed to aid the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan}} in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon}} File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The {{ill|Bracket (architecture)|en|Bracket (architecture)|WD=Q2465790|lt=bracket}}s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
