[[arz:ساجامى جى]][[co:commons:Category:Sagamiji]][[de:Sagami-ji]][[en:Sagami-ji]][[es:Sagami-ji]][[fr:Sagami-ji]][[ja:酒見寺]][[tr:Sagami-ji]][[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]][[zh:酒見寺]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]][[co:commons:Category:Sagamiji]][[de:Sagami-ji]][[en:Sagami-ji]][[es:Sagami-ji]][[fr:Sagami-ji]][[ja:酒見寺]][[tr:Sagami-ji]][[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]][[zh:酒見寺]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]][[sn:commons:Category:Sagamiji]][[de:Sagami-ji]][[en:Sagami-ji]][[es:Sagami-ji]][[fr:Sagami-ji]][[ja:酒見寺]][[tr:Sagami-ji]][[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]][[zh:酒見寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Sagamiji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182}}|prefecture={{ill|Kasai|WD=Q851155}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.9357|N|134.82959167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664}}|deity={{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3137229]] Wikidata name: Sagami-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai-shi}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo-ken}}, 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Ekādaśamukha}} | country = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhist}} temple in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} era) at the request of {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}, a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} received an oracle from a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}}, {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}, who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple {{ill|Commemorative plaque|en|Commemorative plaque|WD=Q721747|lt=plaques}} record later visits from various {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperors}} and ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}s'', including ''shōgun'' {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. The building was badly damaged in the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji rebellion}} of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji}}, {{ill|Honda Tadamasa|en|Honda Tadamasa|WD=Q3275929|lt=Honda Tadamasa}}, agreed to aid the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan}} in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon}} File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The {{ill|Bracket (architecture)|en|Bracket (architecture)|WD=Q2465790|lt=bracket}}s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai-shi}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo-ken}}, 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Ekādaśamukha}} | country = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhist}} temple in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} era) at the request of {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}, a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} received an oracle from a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}}, {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}, who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple {{ill|Commemorative plaque|en|Commemorative plaque|WD=Q721747|lt=plaques}} record later visits from various {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperors}} and ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}s'', including ''shōgun'' {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. The building was badly damaged in the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji rebellion}} of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji}}, {{ill|Honda Tadamasa|en|Honda Tadamasa|WD=Q3275929|lt=Honda Tadamasa}}, agreed to aid the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan}} in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon}} File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The {{ill|Bracket (architecture)|en|Bracket (architecture)|WD=Q2465790|lt=bracket}}s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} era) at the request of {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}, a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} received an oracle from a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}}, {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}, who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple {{ill|Commemorative plaque|en|Commemorative plaque|WD=Q721747|lt=plaques}} record later visits from various {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperors}} and ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}s'', including ''shōgun'' {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. The building was badly damaged in the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji rebellion}} of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji}}, {{ill|Honda Tadamasa|en|Honda Tadamasa|WD=Q3275929|lt=Honda Tadamasa}}, agreed to aid the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan}} in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon}} File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The {{ill|Bracket (architecture)|en|Bracket (architecture)|WD=Q2465790|lt=bracket}}s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

Inscriptions on temple {{ill|Commemorative plaque|en|Commemorative plaque|WD=Q721747|lt=plaques|1=Commemorative plaque}} record later visits from various {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperors|1=Emperor of Japan}} and ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}s'', including ''shōgun'' {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji Domain}}, {{ill|Honda Tadamasa|en|Honda Tadamasa|WD=Q3275929|lt=Honda Tadamasa|1=Honda Tadamasa}}, agreed to aid the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}} in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon|1=Rōmon}} File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The {{ill|Bracket (architecture)|en|Bracket (architecture)|WD=Q2465790|lt=bracket|1=Bracket (architecture)}}s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[arz:ساجامى جى]][[co:commons:Category:Sagamiji]][[de:Sagami-ji]][[en:Sagami-ji]][[es:Sagami-ji]][[fr:Sagami-ji]][[ja:酒見寺]][[tr:Sagami-ji]][[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]][[zh:酒見寺]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]][[co:commons:Category:Sagamiji]][[de:Sagami-ji]][[en:Sagami-ji]][[es:Sagami-ji]][[fr:Sagami-ji]][[ja:酒見寺]][[tr:Sagami-ji]][[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]][[zh:酒見寺]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]][[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sagamiji]][[de:Sagami-ji]][[en:Sagami-ji]][[es:Sagami-ji]][[fr:Sagami-ji]][[ja:酒見寺]][[tr:Sagami-ji]][[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]][[zh:酒見寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Sagamiji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}}|prefecture={{ill|Kasai|WD=Q851155|1=Kasai, Hyōgo}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.9357|N|134.82959167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}}|deity={{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731|1=Ekādaśamukha}}}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai-shi|1=Kasai, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo-ken|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Ekādaśamukha|1=Ekādaśamukha}} | country = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhist|1=Buddhism}} temple in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai|1=Kasai, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} era) at the request of {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}}, a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} received an oracle from a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple {{ill|Commemorative plaque|en|Commemorative plaque|WD=Q721747|lt=plaques|1=Commemorative plaque}} record later visits from various {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperors|1=Emperor of Japan}} and ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}s'', including ''shōgun'' {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. The building was badly damaged in the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji rebellion|1=Heiji rebellion}} of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji Domain}}, {{ill|Honda Tadamasa|en|Honda Tadamasa|WD=Q3275929|lt=Honda Tadamasa|1=Honda Tadamasa}}, agreed to aid the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}} in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon|1=Rōmon}} File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The {{ill|Bracket (architecture)|en|Bracket (architecture)|WD=Q2465790|lt=bracket|1=Bracket (architecture)}}s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[arz:ساجامى جى]][[co:commons:Category:Sagamiji]][[de:Sagami-ji]][[en:Sagami-ji]][[es:Sagami-ji]][[fr:Sagami-ji]][[ja:酒見寺]][[tr:Sagami-ji]][[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]][[zh:酒見寺]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]][[co:commons:Category:Sagamiji]][[de:Sagami-ji]][[en:Sagami-ji]][[es:Sagami-ji]][[fr:Sagami-ji]][[ja:酒見寺]][[tr:Sagami-ji]][[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]][[zh:酒見寺]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]][[de:Sagami-ji]][[en:Sagami-ji]][[es:Sagami-ji]][[fr:Sagami-ji]][[ja:酒見寺]][[tr:Sagami-ji]][[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]][[zh:酒見寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Sagamiji03s3200.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}}|prefecture={{ill|Kasai|WD=Q851155|1=Kasai, Hyōgo}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.9357|N|134.82959167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}}|deity={{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731|1=Ekādaśamukha}}}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Sagami-ji
{{lang|ja-Hani|酒見寺}} | native_name = | image = Sagamiji03s3200.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = A ''[[tahōtō]]'' pagoda at Sagami-ji | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 1319 Hōjō-chō, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai-shi|1=Kasai, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo-ken|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, 675-2312 | coordinates = | religious_affiliation = [[Shingon|Kōyasan Shingon]] | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Ekādaśamukha|1=Ekādaśamukha}} | country = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | functional_status = | website = | founded_by = {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} | year_completed = 745 }} {{nihongo|'''Sagami-ji'''|酒見寺|| or ''Sagami-dera''}}, is a [[Shingon]] {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhist|1=Buddhism}} temple in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai|1=Kasai, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Its mountain name (''[[Buddhist temples in Japan#sangō|sangō]]'') is {{nihongo|Senjōsan|泉生山}}. {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} ordered its construction in 745 (the 17th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} era) at the request of {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}}, a Buddhist priest. == History == According to the temple records, the priest {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} received an oracle from a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, {{nihongo|Sagami Myōjin|酒見明神}}, instructing a temple to be built on these grounds. Gyōki took the request to {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, who then ordered the construction of Sagami-ji. When finished in 745, it was named ''Sagami'' after the oracle's origins. Inscriptions on temple {{ill|Commemorative plaque|en|Commemorative plaque|WD=Q721747|lt=plaques|1=Commemorative plaque}} record later visits from various {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperors|1=Emperor of Japan}} and ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}s'', including ''shōgun'' {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. The building was badly damaged in the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji rebellion|1=Heiji rebellion}} of 1159, and later rebuilt. The main temple was burnt down in conflicts during 1578, and was not rebuilt until the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji Domain}}, {{ill|Honda Tadamasa|en|Honda Tadamasa|WD=Q3275929|lt=Honda Tadamasa|1=Honda Tadamasa}}, agreed to aid the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}} in its reconstruction. == Architecture == {{Empty section|date=January 2012}} == images == File:Sagamiji01s3200.jpg|{{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|WD=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon|1=Rōmon}} File:Sagamiji02s3200.jpg|Entrance File:Sagamiji07s3200.jpg|Courtyard File:Sagamiji05s3200.jpg|The [[shōrō]] (belfry) File:Sagami Temple 2600px.jpg|Shōrō closeup File:Sagamiji04s3200.jpg|The {{ill|Bracket (architecture)|en|Bracket (architecture)|WD=Q2465790|lt=bracket|1=Bracket (architecture)}}s of the ''[[tahōtō]]'' File:Sagamiji08s3200.jpg|Main temple path == External links == {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sagamiji}} {{coord|34|56|8.52|N|134|49|46.53|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[en:Sagami-ji]] [[arz:ساجامى جى]] {{Commons category|Sagamiji}} [[de:Sagami-ji]] [[es:Sagami-ji]] [[fr:Sagami-ji]] [[ja:酒見寺]] [[tr:Sagami-ji]] [[vi:Tửu Kiến tự]] [[zh:酒見寺]] {{wikidata link|Q3137229}} [[Category:745 establishments]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Pagodas in Japan]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 740s]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
